title: Jack Henry & Associates, Inc. (JKHY) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 73/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6% growth rate reflects the mature nature of the core banking software market. Growth is primarily driven by methodical cross-selling, steady pricing increases, and a slow shift toward more profitable, cloud-hosted revenue streams, rather than rapid new client acquisition.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8% discount rate acknowledges the incredibly defensive, highly predictable nature of Jack Henry's recurring revenue base. The near-zero risk of mass customer defection justifies a lower risk premium, despite the concentration risk within the regional banking sector.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate assumes the company will grow roughly in line with the long-term inflation and GDP growth of the US economy, constrained by the slow, ongoing consolidation of the community banking market.

thesisParagraphs: Jack Henry is the quintessential 'sleep well at night' stock. It provides the mission-critical, deeply embedded software that keeps hundreds of US regional and community banks functioning. Because replacing a core banking system is a massive, multi-year, high-risk endeavor, Jack Henry enjoys customer retention rates that border on absolute lock-in. This translates to incredibly stable, predictable, and highly profitable recurring revenue.

However, this phenomenal business quality is a known quantity, and the market prices it accordingly. The stock rarely trades at what a traditional value investor would consider a bargain. My DCF model, utilizing realistic growth rates for a mature market, indicates the stock is currently overvalued. Investors buying at these levels are paying a significant premium for the certainty and defensive characteristics of the cash flows, rather than buying at a true margin of safety.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is the FCF growth rate for Jack Henry only 6%?
faq.answer: Jack Henry operates in a highly mature, heavily consolidated market. The number of community banks in the US is slowly shrinking, meaning growth must come from selling more services to existing clients and gradual price increases, which inherently caps growth at a moderate, single-digit pace.

faq.question: Why does the model say JKHY is overvalued despite being a great business?
faq.answer: A great business does not automatically equal a great investment at any price. The market clearly recognizes Jack Henry's immense moat and predictable cash flows, and it has priced the stock with a significant 'quality premium' that exceeds the mathematical output of a standard DCF model.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.

prospectVerdictDetail: Jack Henry maintains an incredibly durable economic moat within the regional and community banking sector. Providing the core technological infrastructure for financial institutions creates extremely high switching costs and sticky, recurring revenue. While growth is rarely explosive in this mature, consolidated market, the company's consistent execution, modernization efforts, and steady margin profile make it a remarkably stable compounder.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Jack Henry operates in a mature oligopoly. Its competitive momentum is driven by consistent, albeit moderate, organic growth and a relentless focus on customer retention and cross-selling within its established base.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Revenue growth is steady, typically in the mid-to-high single digits (currently around 7.9%). This is consistent with peers like Fiserv and FIS in the core processing space, reflecting a mature market rather than hyper-growth.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Market share shifts are glacial due to the massive friction of changing core systems. Jack Henry holds a strong position among community and regional banks, rarely losing clients, but blockbuster new wins are equally infrequent.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company possesses solid pricing power, routinely passing along inflationary increases. However, the consolidated nature of the vendor market and the cost-consciousness of smaller banks limit aggressive price hikes.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The modernization of legacy core systems is a slow, methodical process. Jack Henry is investing heavily in cloud-native technology and open APIs, but the pace of adoption by its conservative customer base is measured.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Jack Henry's moat is exceptionally wide. It is built entirely on the massive operational risk and financial cost associated with a bank ripping out and replacing its core processing system.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Core systems are the nervous system of a bank. Changing providers takes years, costs millions, and carries immense operational risk. Client retention rates routinely hover near 99%, indicating near-absolute lock-in.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While true network effects are weak, a localized ecosystem effect exists. As Jack Henry builds out its open API platform (Banno), more third-party fintechs integrate with it, making the overall platform stickier for the bank.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Banking is intensely regulated. Jack Henry assumes significant compliance burdens for its clients, acting as a critical buffer. This expertise and shared regulatory infrastructure represent a powerful competitive advantage against new entrants.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a pure software and services company, capital intensity is low. The shift toward hosted/cloud delivery models further streamlines operations and supports very high, predictable free cash flow generation.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is stable, reflecting the stock's defensive characteristics. Investors appreciate the predictable revenue streams amidst broader macroeconomic uncertainty.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Earnings estimates remain stable and slightly upward-trending, supported by high recurring revenue (over 80%) and a multi-year backlog of committed contracts that buffer against short-term economic shocks.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative focuses on the company's defensive qualities. While regional banking stress (e.g., the 2023 crisis) caused temporary jitters, Jack Henry's client base has proven resilient, and consolidation often works to the benefit of larger core providers.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management is highly disciplined, focusing on slow, steady compounding. Capital allocation is conservative, prioritizing internal R&D (technology modernization), small tuck-in acquisitions, and a steadily growing dividend.

prospectKeyRisks: Continued consolidation in the regional and community banking sector shrinks the total addressable market and reduces the total number of core processing contracts.
A failure to modernize legacy systems quickly enough could result in clients migrating to newer, cloud-native core providers, despite the high switching costs.
Macroeconomic shocks or severe regional bank failures that significantly impair the financial health of its core customer base.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Accelerated adoption of its modernized, cloud-native platform architecture, driving higher recurring revenue and improved margins.
Increased demand for integrated digital banking and payments solutions as community banks try to compete with national megabanks.
Accretive tuck-in acquisitions that expand its product suite and allow for further cross-selling into its massive, captive client base.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored JKHY at 75/100 and Opus at 75/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
